26	// KindTaskEnvelopeIn is the "kind" discriminator for input envelopes written
27	// by the orchestrator and consumed by subagent images.
28	const KindTaskEnvelopeIn = "TaskEnvelopeIn"
29	
30	// KindTaskEnvelopeOut is the "kind" discriminator for output envelopes written
31	// by the in-pod harness and consumed by the controller on Job completion.
32	const KindTaskEnvelopeOut = "TaskEnvelopeOut"
33	
34	// EnvelopeIn is the self-contained input document written by the orchestrator
35	// to /workspace/envelopes/{task-uid}/in.json on the per-Project PVC. The
36	// subagent pod mounts the PVC read-only; everything the agent needs to run is
37	// in this document (D-A4). Out-of-tree subagent image authors decode this type
38	// via [ValidateAPIVersionKind] + json.Unmarshal.
39	type EnvelopeIn struct {
40		// APIVersion is pinned to [APIVersionV1Alpha1]. Harness MUST reject any
41		// other value via [ValidateAPIVersionKind] before consuming any field.
42		APIVersion string `json:"apiVersion"`
43	
44		// Kind is [KindTaskEnvelopeIn]. Harness MUST reject any other value.
45		Kind string `json:"kind"`
46	
47		// TaskUID is the Kubernetes UID of the owning Task CRD object. Carried
48		// through to EnvelopeOut so the controller correlates result envelopes
49		// without scanning the PVC directory tree.
50		TaskUID string `json:"taskUID"`
51	
52		// Role is the planner/executor selector: "planner" or "executor".
53		Role string `json:"role"`
54	
55		// Level is the hierarchy level this task operates at:
56		// "milestone" | "phase" | "plan" | "task".
57		Level string `json:"level"`
58	
59		// Prompt is the full prompt body the subagent should act on.
60		// For executor dispatches this field is empty — the executor reads the
61		// prompt in-pod from PromptPath off its own namespace PVC (defect #10b).
62		// For planner dispatches (role="planner") this field carries the resolved
63		// outcome prompt directly (the outcome prompt is not a PVC artifact).
64		Prompt string `json:"prompt"`
65	
66		// PromptPath is the workspace-relative path of the executor prompt artifact
67		// on the per-Project namespace PVC (e.g.
68		// "envelopes/<plannerUID>/children/task-01.json"). Non-empty only for
69		// executor dispatches (role="executor"). The in-pod anthropic runner reads
70		// the .spec.prompt field from this artifact and renders it as {{.Prompt}}
71		// in the compiled-in prompt template. The Manager MUST NOT read this path
72		// cross-namespace; the read happens in-pod so it uses the executor's own
73		// namespace PVC (defect #10b). Traversal-defended in-pod: absolute paths
74		// and "../" escapes are rejected before the read.
75		PromptPath string `json:"promptPath,omitempty"`
76	
77		// Branch is the per-run git branch (project.Status.Git.BranchName, format
78		// "tide/run-<project>-<unix>") the executor checks out its isolated worktree
79		// onto. Non-empty only for executor dispatches (role="executor"); planner
80		// dispatches short-circuit worktree creation (harness.EnsureWorktree). The
81		// in-pod executor reads this from in.json — the single source of truth for
82		// the worktree branch (09-09: replaced the never-written branch.txt).
83		Branch string `json:"branch,omitempty"`
84	
85		// FilesTouched is the set of repo-relative paths this task is expected to
86		// read or write. Used by the harness for output-path validation (HARN-05)
87		// and by the Plan admission webhook for file-touch consistency checks
88		// (PLAN-02).
89		FilesTouched []string `json:"filesTouched"`
90	
91		// DependsOn is the list of sibling Task names this task declares as
92		// predecessors in the execution DAG. Omitted when empty (D-A4).
93		DependsOn []string `json:"dependsOn,omitempty"`
94	
95		// DeclaredOutputPaths is the exhaustive list of PVC-relative paths the
96		// harness is permitted to write. Any write outside this set is rejected by
97		// the harness (HARN-05).
98		DeclaredOutputPaths []string `json:"declaredOutputPaths"`
99	
100		// Caps carries per-task resource limits enforced by the in-pod harness

100		// Caps carries per-task resource limits enforced by the in-pod harness
101		// (HARN-02). The orchestrator derives these from Project.Spec.budget.
102		Caps Caps `json:"caps"`
103	
104		// ProxyEndpoint is the localhost HTTPS endpoint for the signed-token
105		// credential proxy sidecar (D-C1). Subagent sets ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL to
106		// this value; the sidecar holds the real API key.
107		ProxyEndpoint string `json:"proxyEndpoint"`
108	
109		// SignedToken is the HMAC-SHA256 token minted by the controller at
110		// Job-create time. The credential proxy validates this token before
111		// forwarding API requests (D-C3).
112		SignedToken string `json:"signedToken"`
113	
114		// Provider selects the LLM vendor + model + per-vendor tuning knobs the
115		// subagent image should use to satisfy this dispatch (D-C3). Value type
116		// (not pointer): every dispatch declares a provider. The dispatching
117		// reconciler resolves Project.Spec.subagent.levels.{level}.{vendor,model,
118		// params} → Project-level defaults → Helm-chart defaults and stamps the
119		// resolved triple here. The provider implementation in
120		// internal/subagent/{vendor}/ rejects an envelope whose Provider.Vendor
121		// does not match its compiled-in sentinel — fail-fast defense against
122		// image-tag-vs-envelope drift.
123		//
124		// Provider.Params carries MODEL parameters only (temperature, thinking_budget,
125		// …). It is NOT a general metadata bag — the anthropic runner hard-rejects
126		// any key outside its allow-list. Dispatch metadata the planner needs to
127		// build child-CRD refs lives in [EnvelopeIn.Dispatch], not here.
128		Provider ProviderSpec `json:"provider"`
129	
130		// Dispatch carries orchestrator-side dispatch metadata the planner subagent
131		// reads to build child-CRD references (e.g. parentName → projectRef /
132		// milestoneRef / phaseRef / planRef) without querying the K8s API. It is
133		// distinct from Provider.Params, which is model-parameters-only: overloading
134		// Provider.Params with dispatch metadata tripped the anthropic runner's
135		// strict param allow-list. Pointer + omitempty so executor-level and
136		// real-Claude dispatches that don't consume it don't serialize a
137		// "dispatch: null" placeholder. Real Claude-backed subagent images may
138		// ignore this struct entirely — the real planner authors from the outcome
139		// prompt, not canned parent refs.
140		Dispatch *DispatchMeta `json:"dispatch,omitempty"`
141	
142		// Dev carries test-fixture-only metadata injected by integration tests.
143		// Real Claude-backed subagent images MUST ignore this struct entirely
144		// (RESEARCH.md Pitfall 9 / D-F1). The field is omitted from JSON when nil
145		// so production envelopes are not polluted with "dev: null".
146		Dev *Dev `json:"dev,omitempty"`
147	}
148	
149	// EnvelopeOut is the result document written by the in-pod harness to
150	// /workspace/envelopes/{task-uid}/out.json. The controller reads this file
151	// after the K8s Job terminates to record Task completion status and budget
152	// tally (D-A2).
153	type EnvelopeOut struct {
154		// APIVersion is pinned to [APIVersionV1Alpha1].
155		APIVersion string `json:"apiVersion"`
156	
157		// Kind is [KindTaskEnvelopeOut].
158		Kind string `json:"kind"`
159	
160		// TaskUID mirrors EnvelopeIn.TaskUID so the controller can correlate
161		// result envelopes without scanning directory names.
162		TaskUID string `json:"taskUID"`
163	
164		// ExitCode is the subagent process exit code: 0 = success, non-zero = failure.
165		ExitCode int `json:"exitCode"`
166	
167		// Result is a human-readable one-line summary of the task outcome. Written
168		// by the harness on success or by the stub's canned response on testMode.
169		Result string `json:"result"`
170	
171		// Reason carries a structured failure code when ExitCode != 0, e.g.
172		// "forced-failure", "cap-hit", "output-path-violation", "token-expired".
173		Reason string `json:"reason"`
174	
175		// Usage is the token and cost tally for this task, rolled up by the
176		// controller into Project.Status.budget at Task completion (D-D2).
177		Usage Usage `json:"usage"`
178	
179		// Artifacts is the list of PVC-relative paths the harness confirmed were
180		// written during this task's execution. Used for audit and for the
181		// declared-vs-actual output-path comparison (HARN-05).
182		Artifacts []string `json:"artifacts"`
183	
184		// CompletedAt is the wall-clock time the harness wrote this envelope.
185		CompletedAt time.Time `json:"completedAt"`
186	
187		// ChildCRDs is the slice of typed child-CRD declarations a planner
188		// subagent emits for the orchestrator to materialize server-side (D-A1).
189		// MilestoneReconciler reads Phase entries here; PhaseReconciler reads
190		// Plan entries; PlanReconciler reads Task + Wave entries; TaskReconciler
191		// (executor level) emits no ChildCRDs. Omitted from JSON when empty so
192		// executor-level EnvelopeOut documents stay small. Consumers MUST
193		// validate ChildCRDSpec.Kind against an allowlist before server-side
194		// create — threat T-308 mitigation lives at the consumer site (plan
195		// 03-08), not in this type.
196		ChildCRDs []ChildCRDSpec `json:"childCRDs,omitempty"`
197	
198		// Git carries git-side output a push Job or a harness produces, in
199		// particular the HeadSHA the per-Project run branch was advanced to at
200		// this dispatch's level boundary. Pointer + omitempty so dispatches that
201		// don't touch git (e.g. planner Jobs whose output is consumed only via
202		// child-CRD materialization) don't serialize a "git: null" placeholder.
203		Git *GitOutput `json:"git,omitempty"`
204	
205		// ChildCount is the number of child CRDs the planner authored. When
206		// reading the tiny termination-message status (PodStatusEnvelopeReader),
207		// this field is populated from the TerminationStub.ChildCount JSON key so
208		// planner controllers can perform race-free succession gating without also
209		// carrying the full ChildCRDs payloads (plan 09-08 Defect B). Zero for
210		// executor-level dispatches and genuine leaf planners. When reading a full
211		// out.json via FilesystemEnvelopeReader the field falls back to
212		// len(ChildCRDs) in the planner controllers (non-Option-C path).
213		ChildCount int `json:"childCount,omitempty"`
214	}
215	
216	// GitOutput carries git-side output fields a dispatch produces, populated by
217	// the push Job at level-boundary push success and by executor harnesses
218	// committing per-Task worktree changes (D-B4 / D-B6). HeadSHA is the SHA the
219	// per-run branch was advanced to; ProjectReconciler patches
220	// Project.Status.git.lastPushedSHA from this value to enable the next push's
221	// --force-with-lease check (Pitfall 13 mitigation).
222	type GitOutput struct {
223		// HeadSHA is the 40-character hex SHA the per-run branch points to after
224		// this dispatch's level-boundary push completed. Required when Git is
225		// non-nil.
226		HeadSHA string `json:"headSHA"`
227	}
228	
229	// Caps carries per-task resource limits (HARN-02). The orchestrator derives
230	// these from Project.Spec.budget and injects them into every EnvelopeIn so
231	// the in-pod harness can enforce them without querying the K8s API.
232	type Caps struct {
233		// WallClockSeconds is the maximum wall-clock seconds the subagent is
234		// permitted to run before the harness sends SIGTERM.
235		WallClockSeconds int `json:"wallClockSeconds"`
236	
237		// Iterations is the maximum number of agent loop iterations before the
238		// harness sends SIGTERM.
239		Iterations int `json:"iterations"`
240	
241		// InputTokens is the maximum cumulative input tokens across all LLM calls.
242		InputTokens int64 `json:"inputTokens"`
243	
244		// OutputTokens is the maximum cumulative output tokens across all LLM
245		// calls in this task.
246		OutputTokens int64 `json:"outputTokens"`
247	}
248	
249	// Usage carries the token and cost tally reported by the harness in
250	// EnvelopeOut. Rolled up by the controller into Project.Status.budget at Task
251	// completion (D-D2).
252	type Usage struct {
253		// InputTokens is the total input tokens consumed across all LLM calls
254		// during this task's execution.
255		InputTokens int64 `json:"inputTokens"`
256	
257		// OutputTokens is the total output tokens produced across all LLM calls
258		// during this task's execution.
259		OutputTokens int64 `json:"outputTokens"`
260	
261		// EstimatedCostCents is the estimated US-cent cost for this task, rounded
262		// up to the nearest cent. Used for Project.Status.budget.costSpentCents
263		// rollup.
264		EstimatedCostCents int64 `json:"estimatedCostCents"`
265	
266		// Iterations is the actual number of agent loop iterations completed.
267		Iterations int `json:"iterations"`
268	
269		// CacheReadTokens is the count of input tokens served from the provider's
270		// prompt cache (D-C5). The Anthropic stream-json
271		// usage.cache_read_input_tokens field maps directly here. Surfacing this
272		// separately lets Project.Status.budget (Phase 2 D-D2) show the cached
273		// vs uncached input mix instead of hiding cache hits inside InputTokens.
274		CacheReadTokens int64 `json:"cacheReadTokens"`
275	
276		// CacheCreationTokens is the count of input tokens spent populating the
277		// provider's prompt cache during this dispatch (D-C5). The Anthropic
278		// stream-json usage.cache_creation_input_tokens field maps directly here.
279		// Cache creation is billed at a higher rate than cache reads; separating
280		// the two prevents budget rollups from understating real spend on
281		// cache-warmup turns.
282		CacheCreationTokens int64 `json:"cacheCreationTokens"`
283	}
284	
285	// DispatchMeta carries orchestrator-side dispatch metadata the planner subagent
286	// reads to build child-CRD references without querying the K8s API. It is kept
287	// separate from [ProviderSpec.Params] (model-parameters-only) so the anthropic
288	// runner's strict param allow-list is not tripped by dispatch metadata. Real
289	// Claude-backed subagent images may ignore this struct — the real planner
290	// authors child CRDs from the outcome prompt, not canned parent refs.
291	type DispatchMeta struct {
292		// ParentName is the up-stack CRD's metadata.name. The stub planner stamps
293		// it into the child CRD's parent-spec ref (projectRef / milestoneRef /
294		// phaseRef / planRef) so the controller can wire ownership without the
295		// subagent holding a K8s client (T-07-03-03: parentName is metadata, not a
296		// secret).
297		ParentName string `json:"parentName,omitempty"`
298	}
299	
300	// Dev carries test-fixture-only metadata for the stub-subagent image. Real
301	// Claude-backed subagent images MUST ignore this struct entirely (D-F1 /
302	// RESEARCH.md Pitfall 9). It is intentionally kept in a sub-struct so the
303	// compiler makes omission explicit (pointer + omitempty on EnvelopeIn.Dev) and
304	// grep for "Dev" in production code is unambiguous.
305	type Dev struct {
306		// TestMode is the stub behavior selector. Valid values:
307		//   "success"              — writes canned result.json + declared artifacts; exit 0.
308		//   "fail-exit-1"          — writes structured failure result; exit 1.
309		//   "hang"                 — sleeps past caps.wallClockSeconds (HARN-02 test).
310		//   "exceed-output-paths"  — writes outside declaredOutputPaths (HARN-05 test).
311		// An empty string is treated as "success" by the stub.
312		TestMode string `json:"testMode,omitempty"`
313	}
314	
315	// TerminationStub is the tiny cross-namespace status carrier written to the
316	// dispatch Job's 4 KB termination message (Pod.status.containerStatuses[].
317	// state.terminated.message). It carries ONLY the fields the Manager needs to
318	// record Task completion without reading the PVC: ExitCode, Reason, Usage,
319	// the HeadSHA the run-branch was advanced to (if any), and ChildCount — the
320	// count of child CRDs the planner authored. ChildCRDs payloads and the verbose
321	// Result are intentionally excluded — they stay on the namespace-local PVC in
322	// out.json, which the in-namespace reporter Job reads (plan 09-05).
323	//
324	// ChildCount enables race-free succession gating (plan 09-08 Defect B): the
325	// controller compares observed owned children against this expected count and
326	// requeues until observed >= expected before declaring the level Succeeded.
327	//
328	// By construction this type MUST stay < 4 KB when marshalled; the test
329	// TestNewTerminationStub_StaysSmall enforces this invariant.
330	type TerminationStub struct {
331		// ExitCode mirrors EnvelopeOut.ExitCode: 0 = success, non-zero = failure.
332		ExitCode int `json:"exitCode"`
333	
334		// Reason mirrors EnvelopeOut.Reason: structured failure code (e.g.
335		// "forced-failure", "cap-hit") when ExitCode != 0.
336		Reason string `json:"reason"`
337	
338		// Usage mirrors EnvelopeOut.Usage: the token and cost tally for this
339		// dispatch, rolled up by the Manager into Project.Status.budget.
340		Usage Usage `json:"usage"`
341	
342		// HeadSHA is the git HeadSHA from EnvelopeOut.Git.HeadSHA, flattened here
343		// to avoid a nested pointer. Empty when the dispatch did not produce a git
344		// push (executor-level, non-push Jobs). The Manager copies this into
345		// Project.Status.git.lastPushedSHA for the --force-with-lease fence.
346		HeadSHA string `json:"headSHA,omitempty"`
347	
348		// ChildCount is the number of child CRDs the planner authored
349		// (len(EnvelopeOut.ChildCRDs)). Used by the four planner-level controllers
350		// as the "expected" child count for race-free succession gating: a level
351		// cannot Succeed until observed owned children >= ChildCount (plan 09-08).
352		// Zero for executor-level dispatches and genuine leaf planners.
353		ChildCount int `json:"childCount"`
354	}
355	
356	// NewTerminationStub builds a TerminationStub from a full EnvelopeOut by
357	// copying the tiny-status subset: ExitCode, Reason, Usage, Git.HeadSHA, and
358	// ChildCount (= len(out.ChildCRDs)). ChildCRD payloads, Result, and Artifacts
359	// are deliberately excluded. A nil out.Git is safe and produces an empty
360	// HeadSHA. A nil or empty ChildCRDs slice produces ChildCount == 0.
361	func NewTerminationStub(out EnvelopeOut) TerminationStub {
362		headSHA := ""
363		if out.Git != nil {
364			headSHA = out.Git.HeadSHA
365		}
366		return TerminationStub{
367			ExitCode:   out.ExitCode,
368			Reason:     out.Reason,
369			Usage:      out.Usage,
370			HeadSHA:    headSHA,
371			ChildCount: len(out.ChildCRDs),
372		}
373	}
374	
375	// ValidateAPIVersionKind checks that apiVersion equals [APIVersionV1Alpha1]
376	// and that kind equals expectedKind. It is the first call an envelope consumer
377	// must make before accessing any other field (D-A3).
378	//
379	// Returns [*UnknownAPIVersionError] if apiVersion is unrecognized.
380	// Returns [*UnknownKindError] if kind is unrecognized.
381	// Returns nil on success.
382	func ValidateAPIVersionKind(apiVersion, kind, expectedKind string) error {
383		if apiVersion != APIVersionV1Alpha1 {
384			return &UnknownAPIVersionError{APIVersion: apiVersion}
385		}
386		if kind != expectedKind {
387			return &UnknownKindError{Kind: kind}
388		}
389		return nil
390	}

1	# TIDE controller chart values (Phase 1 surface — D-E1).
2	#
3	# This file is the canonical, hand-maintained values.yaml for charts/tide/. The
4	# `make helm-controller` target generates charts/tide/ via helmify, then copies
5	# THIS file over the helmify-emitted charts/tide/values.yaml. Helmify cannot
6	# infer (a) ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/* image registry, (b) the Phase 1 tunables
7	# (plannerConcurrency, executorConcurrency, maxConcurrentReconciles), or (c)
8	# leaderElection block — those are project-level decisions that live here,
9	# version-controlled separately from helmify's regenerated output.
10	#
11	# Phase 5 will extend this with dashboard.enabled, prometheus.serviceMonitor.enabled,
12	# and additional OSS-distribution flags (D-E2). The structure below — helmify-emitted
13	# controllerManager.* keys plus hand-augmented top-level Phase 1 tunables — is the
14	# Phase 5-compatible foundation.
15	
16	# Image refs (D-A2: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/* registry).
17	controllerManager:
18	  manager:
19	    args:
20	    - --metrics-bind-address=:8443
21	    - --config=/etc/tide/config.yaml
22	    - --watch-namespace=$(WATCH_NAMESPACE)
23	    - --leader-elect
24	    - --webhook-cert-path=/tmp/k8s-webhook-server/serving-certs
25	    containerSecurityContext:
26	      allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
27	      capabilities:
28	        drop:
29	        - ALL
30	      readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
31	    env:
32	      # watchNamespace = "" means watch ALL namespaces (cluster-scoped install posture,
33	      # AUTH-02). Set this to a specific namespace for per-namespace install — the
34	      # cmd/manager/main.go --watch-namespace flag wires it through to the Manager's
35	      # cache.ByObject namespace filter.
36	      watchNamespace: ""
37	    image:
38	      repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-controller
39	      tag: ""
40	      pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
41	    podAnnotations: {}
42	    resources:
43	      limits:
44	        cpu: 500m
45	        memory: 128Mi
46	      requests:
47	        cpu: 10m
48	        memory: 64Mi
49	  podSecurityContext:
50	    runAsNonRoot: true
51	    seccompProfile:
52	      type: RuntimeDefault
53	  replicas: 1
54	  serviceAccount:
55	    annotations: {}
56	kubernetesClusterDomain: cluster.local
57	metricsService:
58	  ports:
59	  - name: https
60	    port: 8443
61	    protocol: TCP
62	    targetPort: 8443
63	  type: ClusterIP
64	webhookService:
65	  ports:
66	  - port: 443
67	    protocol: TCP
68	    targetPort: 9443
69	  type: ClusterIP
70	
71	# Phase 1 runtime tunables (D-E1) — rendered into the tide-config ConfigMap which is
72	# mounted at /etc/tide/config.yaml and parsed by internal/config.Load (CTRL-04 / Plan 04).
73	#
74	# Two distinct parallelism budgets per Pitfall 6: planning fans out wide (Milestone/
75	# Phase/Plan reconciles), execution fans out narrow (Wave/Task reconciles). The
76	# crosspool analyzer (cmd/tide-lint) prevents the two budgets from being unified at
77	# compile time; this values surface keeps them separately tunable at deploy time.
78	plannerConcurrency: 16
79	executorConcurrency: 4
80	
81	# Per-Kind controller-runtime MaxConcurrentReconciles. Values match the Plan 04
82	# defaults — see internal/config/defaults.go. Project/Milestone are singletons-per-
83	# parent in the common case (1 is enough); Wave/Task fan out wider.
84	maxConcurrentReconciles:
85	  project: 1
86	  milestone: 1
87	  phase: 2
88	  plan: 4
89	  wave: 8
90	  task: 16
91	
92	# CTRL-03 leader election. Enabled by default; the controller's
93	# --leader-elect arg is appended in controllerManager.manager.args above.
94	#
95	# Phase 3 plan 03-09 (D-D1 chaos-resume tuning): leaseDurationSeconds,
96	# renewDeadlineSeconds, and retryPeriodSeconds flow through to
97	# controller-runtime via TIDE_LEADER_{LEASE,RENEW,RETRY}_SECONDS env vars on
98	# the manager Deployment. Defaults give a single-pod restart ≤25s failover
99	# ceiling. Invariant: lease > renew > retry (controller-runtime validates).
100	leaderElection:
101	  enabled: true
102	  leaseDurationSeconds: 15
103	  renewDeadlineSeconds: 10
104	  retryPeriodSeconds: 2
105	
106	# Phase 2 additions (Plan 12 wiring — D-C3, D-D1, D-D3, Blocker #1/#2/#3 fixes).
107	
108	# Plan admission file-touch validation mode (D-E3 / Plan 11 PlanCustomValidator).
109	# "warn" is the safe Helm default — operators opt into "strict" via Project.Spec or
110	# --set planAdmission.fileTouchMode=strict. Passed to the controller via
111	# --default-file-touch-mode flag in the Deployment args.
112	planAdmission:
113	  fileTouchMode: warn
114	
115	# Default rate-limit parameters for provider credential Secrets (D-D3).
116	# Applied when no Secret annotation or Project.Spec.Providers entry overrides them.
117	# Passed to the controller via --rate-limit-default-rpm and --rate-limit-default-burst flags.
118	rateLimits:
119	  defaults:
120	    requestsPerMinute: 60
121	    tokensPerMinute: 100000  # informational v1 — not yet enforced at the bucket level
122	    burst: 10
123	
124	# Pricing overrides (D-02): per-model price corrections merged OVER the compiled
125	# priceTable at controller startup. Operators use this to correct price drift without
126	# a TIDE release. Keys are exact model ID strings; values are cents per million tokens.
127	# Passed to the controller via --pricing-overrides-json flag (JSON-encoded map).
128	#
129	# Example (all fields optional — omitted fields inherit compiled defaults):
130	#   pricing:
131	#     overrides:
132	#       claude-some-future-model:
133	#         inputCentsPerMTok: 300
134	#         outputCentsPerMTok: 1500
135	#         cacheReadCentsPerMTok: 30
136	#         cacheWriteCentsPerMTok: 375
137	pricing:
138	  overrides: {}
139	
140	# Phase 2 image refs for credproxy sidecar + build/load tooling (D-A2).
141	# images.stubSubagent.* keys are retained for image build/load tooling
142	# (make test-int-kind-prep loads the stub image by tag), but are NO LONGER
143	# injected as a --subagent-image flag into the Deployment args (Phase 13 D-01;
144	# that flag was silently forcing the stub in every v1.0.0 install and has been
145	# dropped). Subagent image default now flows via subagent.defaults.image →
146	# CLAUDE_SUBAGENT_IMAGE env var on the manager Deployment.
147	#
148	# Phase 3 plan 03-09 additions: claudeSubagent (D-C2 real-Claude image, swaps
149	# in alongside stubSubagent; both images stay buildable so chaos-resume and
150	# kind smoke tests keep working with the stub) and tidePush (D-B1 dedicated
151	# push-Job image — orchestrator-side gitleaks + go-git push-with-lease).
152	#
153	# Production reproducibility: tags are mutable in the OCI spec; pin by
154	# `@sha256:<digest>` in production overrides (`--set images.tidePush.tag=...`
155	# with the digest, or set repository: ghcr.io/.../tide-push@sha256:...).
156	images:
157	  stubSubagent:
158	    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-stub-subagent
159	    tag: ""
160	    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
161	  credProxy:
162	    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-credproxy
163	    tag: ""
164	    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
165	  busybox:
166	    repository: busybox
167	    tag: "1.36"
168	    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
169	  # Phase 3 plan 03-09 (D-B1): dedicated tide-push image. Orchestrator-side
170	  # push Job per level boundary; gitleaks + go-git + --force-with-lease.
171	  # Wired to controller via TIDE_PUSH_IMAGE env var on manager Deployment.
172	  tidePush:
173	    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-push
174	    tag: ""
175	    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
176	  # Phase 3 plan 03-09 (D-C2 swap-in): real Claude-Code-backed subagent image.
177	  # Used as the default fallback when Project.Spec.subagent.image is empty;
178	  # individual Projects can pin a different vendor image per Project (D-C2).
179	  # Wired to controller via CLAUDE_SUBAGENT_IMAGE env var on manager Deployment.
180	  # In production, prefer pinning by @sha256 digest — tag-based pulls of mutable tags
181	  # are not reproducible across pulls.
182	  claudeSubagent:
183	    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent
184	    tag: ""
185	    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
186	  # Phase 09 plan 09-06 (REQ-09-01): in-namespace reporter image. The reader Job
187	  # runs in the project namespace as tide-reporter SA, reads out.json from the PVC,
188	  # and materializes child CRDs via the K8s API (Option C architecture).
189	  # Wired to controller via TIDE_REPORTER_IMAGE env var on the manager Deployment.
190	  # When empty, the spawn site skips Job creation (same as TIDE_PUSH_IMAGE skip in
191	  # boundary_push.go). In production, prefer pinning by @sha256 digest.
192	  tideReporter:
193	    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-reporter
194	    tag: ""
195	    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
196	
197	# Phase 3 plan 03-09 (D-C4): per-level subagent defaults. Resolution chain at
198	# dispatch time (in MilestoneReconciler / PhaseReconciler / PlanReconciler before
199	# calling Dispatcher.Run):
200	#
201	#   Levels.<level>.Image (project.Spec.subagent.levels.<level>.image) ← per-level override
202	#   → Spec.Subagent.Image (project.Spec.subagent.image)               ← per-Project default
203	#   → subagent.defaults.image (this value → CLAUDE_SUBAGENT_IMAGE env) ← Helm chart default
204	#
205	#   project.Spec.subagent.levels.<level>.model  ← per-level model override
206	#   project.Spec.subagent.model                 ← per-Project model default
207	#   subagent.defaults.model                     ← Helm chart default (this block)
208	#   subagent.levels.<level>.model               ← Helm per-level default (this block)
209	#
210	# Phase 13 D-01 image posture: production default is the real Claude-backed subagent
211	# (ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent). The stub is explicit opt-in for tests/CI
212	# only — it is never injected implicitly. To use the stub:
213	#   --set subagent.defaults.image=ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-stub-subagent:<tag>
214	# Bare refs (no ":<tag>" or "@sha256:" suffix) have ":<appVersion>" appended by the
215	# chart template. Tag- or digest-qualified values pass through verbatim.
216	# Digest pinning is recommended for production reproducibility:
217	#   --set subagent.defaults.image=ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent@sha256:<digest>
218	#
219	# Per-level model defaults (D-C4) match the phase-brief lock-in:
220	#   milestone → claude-opus-4-7    (heavy planning, lowest fan-out)
221	#   phase     → claude-sonnet-4-6
222	#   plan      → claude-sonnet-4-6
223	#   task      → claude-haiku-4-5   (highest fan-out, cost-bounded)
224	#
225	# Per-level models flow through the manager Deployment env block as
226	# TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_{MILESTONE,PHASE,PLAN,TASK}.
227	subagent:
228	  defaults:
229	    image: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent
230	    model: claude-sonnet-4-6
231	  levels:
232	    milestone:
233	      model: claude-opus-4-7
234	    phase:
235	      model: claude-sonnet-4-6
236	    plan:
237	      model: claude-sonnet-4-6
238	    task:
239	      model: claude-haiku-4-5
240	
241	# Phase 3 plan 03-09 (D-B3): optional cluster-wide gitleaks override.
242	# Empty (default) → push Job uses the compiled-in gitleaks v8 ruleset.
243	# Per-Project overrides are still supported via Project.Spec.git.leaksConfigRef
244	# (D-B3 — ConfigMap mounted into push Job at /etc/tide/gitleaks-config.toml).
245	# This top-level key is the cluster-wide fallback (v1.0 deferred; reserved for v1.x).
246	gitleaks:
247	  configMapName: ""
248	
249	# HMAC signing-secret name (D-C3 / Blocker #1 fix).
250	# The signing-secret.yaml Helm template creates a Secret with this name on first install
251	# using Helm's `lookup` + `if not` guard (idempotent). resource-policy: keep annotation
252	# preserves the Secret across helm upgrade/uninstall so in-flight tokens stay valid
253	# (T-02-12-02 / T-02-12-03 mitigations).
254	# The Secret's data key is TIDE_SIGNING_KEY (env-friendly) so envFrom: [{secretRef: ...}]
255	# auto-populates the TIDE_SIGNING_KEY env var on the Manager Deployment and credproxy sidecar.
256	signingKey:
257	  secretName: tide-signing-key
258	
259	# Budget defaults (D-D2 / FAIL-04). Helm-driven hard caps applied to all Projects that
260	# do not specify per-Project caps in Project.Spec.Budget.
261	budget:
262	  # D-05: flat per-dispatch reservation estimate in cents pre-charged against the budget
263	  # cap at Job creation; bounds in-flight overshoot to estimate error instead of wave width;
264	  # 0 disables pre-charge (cap still enforced at dispatch via the BudgetBlocked condition).
265	  # Passed via --budget-reserve-per-dispatch-cents.
266	  reservePerDispatchCents: 100
267	  defaults:
268	    absoluteCapCents: 10000      # $100.00 default absolute cap
269	    rollingWindowCapCents: 5000  # $50.00 / 15-min rolling window default
270	
271	# Phase 4 additions (Plan 04-14 — D-X3 / D-D2 / D-O6 / D-O3).
272	#
273	# All three blocks are ADDITIVE. The Phase 1 surface above is unchanged
274	# (CLAUDE.md "chart is FIXED contract — binary catches up to chart, never
275	# reverse" anti-pattern). Phase 4 chart additions land here without touching
276	# any pre-Phase-4 keys.
277	
278	# Read-only dashboard backend (Phase 4 D-D2). Ships as a separate
279	# Deployment + Service + ServiceAccount + ClusterRole + ClusterRoleBinding
280	# under templates/dashboard-{deployment,service,rbac}.yaml. The dashboard
281	# ClusterRole is enforced read-only at chart-render time via
282	# `make helm-rbac-assert` (T-04-D2 mitigation).
283	#
284	# Default ON because the dashboard is one of the four Phase 4 deliverables;
285	# operators who want the controller-only install set
286	# `--set dashboard.enabled=false`.
287	dashboard:
288	  enabled: true
289	  image:
290	    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-dashboard
291	    tag: ""             # default to .Chart.AppVersion
292	    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
293	  replicas: 1
294	  resources:
295	    requests:
296	      cpu: 50m
297	      memory: 64Mi
298	    limits:
299	      cpu: 200m
300	      memory: 256Mi
301	  service:
302	    type: ClusterIP
303	    port: 80
304	    targetPort: 8080
305	
306	# Prometheus ServiceMonitor (Phase 4 D-O6).
307	#
308	# DEFAULT OFF per CLAUDE.md anti-pattern:
309	#   "Default the chart's ServiceMonitor to prometheus.enabled=false to avoid
310	#    CRD-not-found on plain clusters."
311	#
312	# Enable explicitly via `--set prometheus.serviceMonitor.enabled=true` once
313	# the prometheus-operator CRDs (monitoring.coreos.com/v1) are installed.
314	prometheus:
315	  # PromQL-proxy endpoint (Phase 4 telemetry surface -- milestone-01 Q1 lock-in).
316	  #
317	  # Q1 DECISION: PROXY, not a second browser-side datasource. This is a
318	  # SERVER-SIDE value. When non-empty it is injected as the PROM_ENDPOINT env
319	  # var into the dashboard container (templates/dashboard-deployment.yaml, landed
320	  # by plan-02), and the dashboard chi server proxies PromQL via
321	  # GET /api/v1/query and GET /api/v1/query_range. The browser therefore never
322	  # learns a second network target: single origin, no CORS, one auth surface,
323	  # and the Prometheus URL stays confined to the dashboard process (matters when
324	  # Prometheus is ClusterIP-only and unreachable from outside the cluster).
325	  #
326	  # Empty (default) => graceful degradation: the proxy returns HTTP 200 with the
327	  # body {"status":"unavailable"} and the React Telemetry tab falls back to
328	  # live-only CRD-.status views (no error state, no blank panel, no broken
329	  # layout). Set e.g.
330	  #   --set prometheus.endpoint=http://prometheus-operated.monitoring:9090
331	  # to wire historical cost-over-time charts.
332	  endpoint: ""
333	  # Prometheus local-TSDB retention window for telemetry history (Phase 4 -- Q3).
334	  #
335	  # This chart ships a ServiceMonitor only (serviceMonitor block below) and does
336	  # NOT bundle a Prometheus server, so this value configures no in-chart
337	  # component. It is the single documented knob for the retention operators must
338	  # apply as --storage.tsdb.retention.time=<value> on their operator-managed
339	  # Prometheus.
340	  #
341	  # RETENTION MATH: one multi-day dogfood run plus a one-week post-run analysis
342	  # window fits comfortably inside the 15d default. For organizations tracking
343	  # cost-over-time trends ACROSS multiple runs (weeks of continuous dogfooding),
344	  # set "30d" or longer so the cost-over-time charts span the full cross-run
345	  # history.
346	  retentionTime: "15d"
347	  serviceMonitor:
348	    enabled: false
349	    interval: 30s
350	    scrapeTimeout: 10s
351	
352	# OpenTelemetry env-driven config (Phase 4 D-O3 / plan 04-03).
353	#
354	# Read by internal/otelinit at controller boot:
355	#   - exporter.endpoint = ""  → no-op TracerProvider (zero overhead; default)
356	#   - exporter.endpoint = "otel-collector:4317" → OTLP gRPC exporter
357	#
358	# Sampler is env-driven (NOT WithSampler in code, per Pitfall 24 mitigation):
359	# OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER + OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER_ARG. Defaults trace 10% of root
360	# spans (`parentbased_traceidratio` arg `0.1`).
361	otel:
362	  exporter:
363	    endpoint: ""
364	  tracesSampler: "parentbased_traceidratio"
365	  tracesSamplerArg: "0.1"
366	  serviceName: "tide-controller-manager"
367	
368	# Single shared RWX PVC for all Projects' workspaces (Blocker #2/#3 fix —
369	# RESEARCH.md Open Question #2 RESOLVED with the single-shared-PVC + subPath architecture).
370	#
371	# Architecture: ONE chart-provisioned tide-projects PVC (ReadWriteMany), Helm-templated at
372	# install time via charts/tide/templates/projects-pvc.yaml.
373	#   - Manager Pod mounts PVC at /workspaces (no subPath) — reads /workspaces/{project-uid}/workspace/...
374	#   - Task Pods mount PVC with subPath: {project-uid}/workspace → appearing as /workspace
375	#   - Init Job (Plan 10) mounts PVC with subPath: {project-uid}/workspace and mkdirs the layout
376	#
377	# Trade-off: single shared PVC means per-Project quota story doesn't fall out of CSI;
378	# quotas are Project.Spec.Budget-driven (FAIL-04) not storage-driven.
379	# Phase 3 ART-02 evaluates per-Project PVCs if/when this becomes a real concern.
380	#
381	# storageClassName: "" uses the cluster-default StorageClass (suitable for kind/minikube/dev).
382	# For production, set to a CSI driver that supports ReadWriteMany (e.g. nfs-csi, ceph-cephfs).
383	workspaces:
384	  pvc:
385	    create: true
386	    name: tide-projects
387	    size: 10Gi
388	    storageClassName: ""  # cluster default; set to a CSI RWX driver for production
389	    accessModes: [ReadWriteMany]  # production-default; matches the chart's single-shared-RWX-PVC architecture (see comment above). Override via `--set workspaces.pvc.accessModes={RWO-mode}` for single-node kind/minikube test clusters whose default provisioner (rancher.io/local-path) only supports the non-shared access modes.
390	
391	# Phase 5 D-X4 — per-namespace RoleBinding template (AUTH-02 catch-up).
392	# Empty default: no per-namespace RoleBindings shipped unless operator opts in.
393	# For multi-Project installs, list the Project namespaces here:
394	#   projectNamespaces:
395	#     - tide-customer-acme
396	#     - tide-customer-globex
397	projectNamespaces: []
398

file.content:
# TIDE controller chart values (Phase 1 surface — D-E1).
#
# This file is the canonical, hand-maintained values.yaml for charts/tide/. The
# `make helm-controller` target generates charts/tide/ via helmify, then copies
# THIS file over the helmify-emitted charts/tide/values.yaml. Helmify cannot
# infer (a) ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/* image registry, (b) the Phase 1 tunables
# (plannerConcurrency, executorConcurrency, maxConcurrentReconciles), or (c)
# leaderElection block — those are project-level decisions that live here,
# version-controlled separately from helmify's regenerated output.
#
# Phase 5 will extend this with dashboard.enabled, prometheus.serviceMonitor.enabled,
# and additional OSS-distribution flags (D-E2). The structure below — helmify-emitted
# controllerManager.* keys plus hand-augmented top-level Phase 1 tunables — is the
# Phase 5-compatible foundation.

# Image refs (D-A2: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/* registry).
controllerManager:
  manager:
    args:
    - --metrics-bind-address=:8443
    - --config=/etc/tide/config.yaml
    - --watch-namespace=$(WATCH_NAMESPACE)
    - --leader-elect
    - --webhook-cert-path=/tmp/k8s-webhook-server/serving-certs
    containerSecurityContext:
      allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
      capabilities:
        drop:
        - ALL
      readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
    env:
      # watchNamespace = "" means watch ALL namespaces (cluster-scoped install posture,
      # AUTH-02). Set this to a specific namespace for per-namespace install — the
      # cmd/manager/main.go --watch-namespace flag wires it through to the Manager's
      # cache.ByObject namespace filter.
      watchNamespace: ""
    image:
      repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-controller
      tag: ""
      pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
    podAnnotations: {}
    resources:
      limits:
        cpu: 500m
        memory: 128Mi
      requests:
        cpu: 10m
        memory: 64Mi
  podSecurityContext:
    runAsNonRoot: true
    seccompProfile:
      type: RuntimeDefault
  replicas: 1
  serviceAccount:
    annotations: {}
kubernetesClusterDomain: cluster.local
metricsService:
  ports:
  - name: https
    port: 8443
    protocol: TCP
    targetPort: 8443
  type: ClusterIP
webhookService:
  ports:
  - port: 443
    protocol: TCP
    targetPort: 9443
  type: ClusterIP

# Phase 1 runtime tunables (D-E1) — rendered into the tide-config ConfigMap which is
# mounted at /etc/tide/config.yaml and parsed by internal/config.Load (CTRL-04 / Plan 04).
#
# Two distinct parallelism budgets per Pitfall 6: planning fans out wide (Milestone/
# Phase/Plan reconciles), execution fans out narrow (Wave/Task reconciles). The
# crosspool analyzer (cmd/tide-lint) prevents the two budgets from being unified at
# compile time; this values surface keeps them separately tunable at deploy time.
plannerConcurrency: 16
executorConcurrency: 4

# Per-Kind controller-runtime MaxConcurrentReconciles. Values match the Plan 04
# defaults — see internal/config/defaults.go. Project/Milestone are singletons-per-
# parent in the common case (1 is enough); Wave/Task fan out wider.
maxConcurrentReconciles:
  project: 1
  milestone: 1
  phase: 2
  plan: 4
  wave: 8
  task: 16

# CTRL-03 leader election. Enabled by default; the controller's
# --leader-elect arg is appended in controllerManager.manager.args above.
#
# Phase 3 plan 03-09 (D-D1 chaos-resume tuning): leaseDurationSeconds,
# renewDeadlineSeconds, and retryPeriodSeconds flow through to
# controller-runtime via TIDE_LEADER_{LEASE,RENEW,RETRY}_SECONDS env vars on
# the manager Deployment. Defaults give a single-pod restart ≤25s failover
# ceiling. Invariant: lease > renew > retry (controller-runtime validates).
leaderElection:
  enabled: true
  leaseDurationSeconds: 15
  renewDeadlineSeconds: 10
  retryPeriodSeconds: 2

# Phase 2 additions (Plan 12 wiring — D-C3, D-D1, D-D3, Blocker #1/#2/#3 fixes).

# Plan admission file-touch validation mode (D-E3 / Plan 11 PlanCustomValidator).
# "warn" is the safe Helm default — operators opt into "strict" via Project.Spec or
# --set planAdmission.fileTouchMode=strict. Passed to the controller via
# --default-file-touch-mode flag in the Deployment args.
planAdmission:
  fileTouchMode: warn

# Default rate-limit parameters for provider credential Secrets (D-D3).
# Applied when no Secret annotation or Project.Spec.Providers entry overrides them.
# Passed to the controller via --rate-limit-default-rpm and --rate-limit-default-burst flags.
rateLimits:
  defaults:
    requestsPerMinute: 60
    tokensPerMinute: 100000  # informational v1 — not yet enforced at the bucket level
    burst: 10

# Pricing overrides (D-02): per-model price corrections merged OVER the compiled
# priceTable at controller startup. Operators use this to correct price drift without
# a TIDE release. Keys are exact model ID strings; values are cents per million tokens.
# Passed to the controller via --pricing-overrides-json flag (JSON-encoded map).
#
# Example (all fields optional — omitted fields inherit compiled defaults):
#   pricing:
#     overrides:
#       claude-some-future-model:
#         inputCentsPerMTok: 300
#         outputCentsPerMTok: 1500
#         cacheReadCentsPerMTok: 30
#         cacheWriteCentsPerMTok: 375
pricing:
  overrides: {}

# Phase 2 image refs for credproxy sidecar + build/load tooling (D-A2).
# images.stubSubagent.* keys are retained for image build/load tooling
# (make test-int-kind-prep loads the stub image by tag), but are NO LONGER
# injected as a --subagent-image flag into the Deployment args (Phase 13 D-01;
# that flag was silently forcing the stub in every v1.0.0 install and has been
# dropped). Subagent image default now flows via subagent.defaults.image →
# CLAUDE_SUBAGENT_IMAGE env var on the manager Deployment.
#
# Phase 3 plan 03-09 additions: claudeSubagent (D-C2 real-Claude image, swaps
# in alongside stubSubagent; both images stay buildable so chaos-resume and
# kind smoke tests keep working with the stub) and tidePush (D-B1 dedicated
# push-Job image — orchestrator-side gitleaks + go-git push-with-lease).
#
# Production reproducibility: tags are mutable in the OCI spec; pin by
# `@sha256:<digest>` in production overrides (`--set images.tidePush.tag=...`
# with the digest, or set repository: ghcr.io/.../tide-push@sha256:...).
images:
  stubSubagent:
    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-stub-subagent
    tag: ""
    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  credProxy:
    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-credproxy
    tag: ""
    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  busybox:
    repository: busybox
    tag: "1.36"
    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  # Phase 3 plan 03-09 (D-B1): dedicated tide-push image. Orchestrator-side
  # push Job per level boundary; gitleaks + go-git + --force-with-lease.
  # Wired to controller via TIDE_PUSH_IMAGE env var on manager Deployment.
  tidePush:
    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-push
    tag: ""
    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  # Phase 3 plan 03-09 (D-C2 swap-in): real Claude-Code-backed subagent image.
  # Used as the default fallback when Project.Spec.subagent.image is empty;
  # individual Projects can pin a different vendor image per Project (D-C2).
  # Wired to controller via CLAUDE_SUBAGENT_IMAGE env var on manager Deployment.
  # In production, prefer pinning by @sha256 digest — tag-based pulls of mutable tags
  # are not reproducible across pulls.
  claudeSubagent:
    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent
    tag: ""
    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  # Phase 09 plan 09-06 (REQ-09-01): in-namespace reporter image. The reader Job
  # runs in the project namespace as tide-reporter SA, reads out.json from the PVC,
  # and materializes child CRDs via the K8s API (Option C architecture).
  # Wired to controller via TIDE_REPORTER_IMAGE env var on the manager Deployment.
  # When empty, the spawn site skips Job creation (same as TIDE_PUSH_IMAGE skip in
  # boundary_push.go). In production, prefer pinning by @sha256 digest.
  tideReporter:
    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-reporter
    tag: ""
    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent

# Phase 3 plan 03-09 (D-C4): per-level subagent defaults. Resolution chain at
# dispatch time (in MilestoneReconciler / PhaseReconciler / PlanReconciler before
# calling Dispatcher.Run):
#
#   Levels.<level>.Image (project.Spec.subagent.levels.<level>.image) ← per-level override
#   → Spec.Subagent.Image (project.Spec.subagent.image)               ← per-Project default
#   → subagent.defaults.image (this value → CLAUDE_SUBAGENT_IMAGE env) ← Helm chart default
#
#   project.Spec.subagent.levels.<level>.model  ← per-level model override
#   project.Spec.subagent.model                 ← per-Project model default
#   subagent.defaults.model                     ← Helm chart default (this block)
#   subagent.levels.<level>.model               ← Helm per-level default (this block)
#
# Phase 13 D-01 image posture: production default is the real Claude-backed subagent
# (ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent). The stub is explicit opt-in for tests/CI
# only — it is never injected implicitly. To use the stub:
#   --set subagent.defaults.image=ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-stub-subagent:<tag>
# Bare refs (no ":<tag>" or "@sha256:" suffix) have ":<appVersion>" appended by the
# chart template. Tag- or digest-qualified values pass through verbatim.
# Digest pinning is recommended for production reproducibility:
#   --set subagent.defaults.image=ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent@sha256:<digest>
#
# Per-level model defaults (D-C4) match the phase-brief lock-in:
#   milestone → claude-opus-4-7    (heavy planning, lowest fan-out)
#   phase     → claude-sonnet-4-6
#   plan      → claude-sonnet-4-6
#   task      → claude-haiku-4-5   (highest fan-out, cost-bounded)
#
# Per-level models flow through the manager Deployment env block as
# TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_{MILESTONE,PHASE,PLAN,TASK}.
subagent:
  defaults:
    image: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent
    model: claude-sonnet-4-6
  levels:
    milestone:
      model: claude-opus-4-7
    phase:
      model: claude-sonnet-4-6
    plan:
      model: claude-sonnet-4-6
    task:
      model: claude-haiku-4-5

# Phase 3 plan 03-09 (D-B3): optional cluster-wide gitleaks override.
# Empty (default) → push Job uses the compiled-in gitleaks v8 ruleset.
# Per-Project overrides are still supported via Project.Spec.git.leaksConfigRef
# (D-B3 — ConfigMap mounted into push Job at /etc/tide/gitleaks-config.toml).
# This top-level key is the cluster-wide fallback (v1.0 deferred; reserved for v1.x).
gitleaks:
  configMapName: ""

# HMAC signing-secret name (D-C3 / Blocker #1 fix).
# The signing-secret.yaml Helm template creates a Secret with this name on first install
# using Helm's `lookup` + `if not` guard (idempotent). resource-policy: keep annotation
# preserves the Secret across helm upgrade/uninstall so in-flight tokens stay valid
# (T-02-12-02 / T-02-12-03 mitigations).
# The Secret's data key is TIDE_SIGNING_KEY (env-friendly) so envFrom: [{secretRef: ...}]
# auto-populates the TIDE_SIGNING_KEY env var on the Manager Deployment and credproxy sidecar.
signingKey:
  secretName: tide-signing-key

# Budget defaults (D-D2 / FAIL-04). Helm-driven hard caps applied to all Projects that
# do not specify per-Project caps in Project.Spec.Budget.
budget:
  # D-05: flat per-dispatch reservation estimate in cents pre-charged against the budget
  # cap at Job creation; bounds in-flight overshoot to estimate error instead of wave width;
  # 0 disables pre-charge (cap still enforced at dispatch via the BudgetBlocked condition).
  # Passed via --budget-reserve-per-dispatch-cents.
  reservePerDispatchCents: 100
  defaults:
    absoluteCapCents: 10000      # $100.00 default absolute cap
    rollingWindowCapCents: 5000  # $50.00 / 15-min rolling window default

# Phase 4 additions (Plan 04-14 — D-X3 / D-D2 / D-O6 / D-O3).
#
# All three blocks are ADDITIVE. The Phase 1 surface above is unchanged
# (CLAUDE.md "chart is FIXED contract — binary catches up to chart, never
# reverse" anti-pattern). Phase 4 chart additions land here without touching
# any pre-Phase-4 keys.

# Read-only dashboard backend (Phase 4 D-D2). Ships as a separate
# Deployment + Service + ServiceAccount + ClusterRole + ClusterRoleBinding
# under templates/dashboard-{deployment,service,rbac}.yaml. The dashboard
# ClusterRole is enforced read-only at chart-render time via
# `make helm-rbac-assert` (T-04-D2 mitigation).
#
# Default ON because the dashboard is one of the four Phase 4 deliverables;
# operators who want the controller-only install set
# `--set dashboard.enabled=false`.
dashboard:
  enabled: true
  image:
    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-dashboard
    tag: ""             # default to .Chart.AppVersion
    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  replicas: 1
  resources:
    requests:
      cpu: 50m
      memory: 64Mi
    limits:
      cpu: 200m
      memory: 256Mi
  service:
    type: ClusterIP
    port: 80
    targetPort: 8080

# Prometheus ServiceMonitor (Phase 4 D-O6).
#
# DEFAULT OFF per CLAUDE.md anti-pattern:
#   "Default the chart's ServiceMonitor to prometheus.enabled=false to avoid
#    CRD-not-found on plain clusters."
#
# Enable explicitly via `--set prometheus.serviceMonitor.enabled=true` once
# the prometheus-operator CRDs (monitoring.coreos.com/v1) are installed.
prometheus:
  # PromQL-proxy endpoint (Phase 4 telemetry surface -- milestone-01 Q1 lock-in).
  #
  # Q1 DECISION: PROXY, not a second browser-side datasource. This is a
  # SERVER-SIDE value. When non-empty it is injected as the PROM_ENDPOINT env
  # var into the dashboard container (templates/dashboard-deployment.yaml, landed
  # by plan-02), and the dashboard chi server proxies PromQL via
  # GET /api/v1/query and GET /api/v1/query_range. The browser therefore never
  # learns a second network target: single origin, no CORS, one auth surface,
  # and the Prometheus URL stays confined to the dashboard process (matters when
  # Prometheus is ClusterIP-only and unreachable from outside the cluster).
  #
  # Empty (default) => graceful degradation: the proxy returns HTTP 200 with the
  # body {"status":"unavailable"} and the React Telemetry tab falls back to
  # live-only CRD-.status views (no error state, no blank panel, no broken
  # layout). Set e.g.
  #   --set prometheus.endpoint=http://prometheus-operated.monitoring:9090
  # to wire historical cost-over-time charts.
  endpoint: ""
  # Prometheus local-TSDB retention window for telemetry history (Phase 4 -- Q3).
  #
  # This chart ships a ServiceMonitor only (serviceMonitor block below) and does
  # NOT bundle a Prometheus server, so this value configures no in-chart
  # component. It is the single documented knob for the retention operators must
  # apply as --storage.tsdb.retention.time=<value> on their operator-managed
  # Prometheus.
  #
  # RETENTION MATH: one multi-day dogfood run plus a one-week post-run analysis
  # window fits comfortably inside the 15d default. For organizations tracking
  # cost-over-time trends ACROSS multiple runs (weeks of continuous dogfooding),
  # set "30d" or longer so the cost-over-time charts span the full cross-run
  # history.
  retentionTime: "15d"
  serviceMonitor:
    enabled: false
    interval: 30s
    scrapeTimeout: 10s

# OpenTelemetry env-driven config (Phase 4 D-O3 / plan 04-03).
#
# Read by internal/otelinit at controller boot:
#   - exporter.endpoint = ""  → no-op TracerProvider (zero overhead; default)
#   - exporter.endpoint = "otel-collector:4317" → OTLP gRPC exporter
#
# Sampler is env-driven (NOT WithSampler in code, per Pitfall 24 mitigation):
# OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER + OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER_ARG. Defaults trace 10% of root
# spans (`parentbased_traceidratio` arg `0.1`).
otel:
  exporter:
    endpoint: ""
  tracesSampler: "parentbased_traceidratio"
  tracesSamplerArg: "0.1"
  serviceName: "tide-controller-manager"

# Single shared RWX PVC for all Projects' workspaces (Blocker #2/#3 fix —
# RESEARCH.md Open Question #2 RESOLVED with the single-shared-PVC + subPath architecture).
#
# Architecture: ONE chart-provisioned tide-projects PVC (ReadWriteMany), Helm-templated at
# install time via charts/tide/templates/projects-pvc.yaml.
#   - Manager Pod mounts PVC at /workspaces (no subPath) — reads /workspaces/{project-uid}/workspace/...
#   - Task Pods mount PVC with subPath: {project-uid}/workspace → appearing as /workspace
#   - Init Job (Plan 10) mounts PVC with subPath: {project-uid}/workspace and mkdirs the layout
#
# Trade-off: single shared PVC means per-Project quota story doesn't fall out of CSI;
# quotas are Project.Spec.Budget-driven (FAIL-04) not storage-driven.
# Phase 3 ART-02 evaluates per-Project PVCs if/when this becomes a real concern.
#
# storageClassName: "" uses the cluster-default StorageClass (suitable for kind/minikube/dev).
# For production, set to a CSI driver that supports ReadWriteMany (e.g. nfs-csi, ceph-cephfs).
workspaces:
  pvc:
    create: true
    name: tide-projects
    size: 10Gi
    storageClassName: ""  # cluster default; set to a CSI RWX driver for production
    accessModes: [ReadWriteMany]  # production-default; matches the chart's single-shared-RWX-PVC architecture (see comment above). Override via `--set workspaces.pvc.accessModes={RWO-mode}` for single-node kind/minikube test clusters whose default provisioner (rancher.io/local-path) only supports the non-shared access modes.

# Phase 5 D-X4 — per-namespace RoleBinding template (AUTH-02 catch-up).
# Empty default: no per-namespace RoleBindings shipped unless operator opts in.
# For multi-Project installs, list the Project namespaces here:
#   projectNamespaces:
#     - tide-customer-acme
#     - tide-customer-globex
projectNamespaces: []

336

339		Budget BudgetConfig `json:"budget,omitempty"`

342		// +optional

344

347		// +kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=1
348		// +kubebuilder:validation:Maximum=10
349		// +optional
350		MaxAttemptsPerTask int32 `json:"maxAttemptsPerTask,omitempty"`
351	
352		// Subagent declares provider+model selection for planner/executor dispatch
353		// (Phase 3 D-C2). When empty, Helm-chart defaults apply.
354		// +optional
355		Subagent SubagentConfig `json:"subagent,omitempty"`
356	
357		// Git declares the per-Project target repo + creds for artifact push
358		// (Phase 3 D-B6). Required for any Project whose lifecycle reaches push;
359		// optional in v1.0 only for purely transient/test Projects.
360		// Pointer so omitempty fully elides the field when absent — value-type
361		// GitConfig{} would serialize as `git: {repoURL: ""}` and trip the
362		// RepoURL pattern validation on existing Phase 2 test fixtures.
363		// +optional
364		Git *GitConfig `json:"git,omitempty"`
365	}
366	
367	// Project Phase constants for Project.Status.Phase (Plan 10 — init Job + budget gate).
368	const (
369		// PhasePending is the initial phase before any reconcile has run.
370		PhasePending = "Pending"
371		// PhaseInitialized is set when the init Job completes successfully.
372		PhaseInitialized = "Initialized"
373		// PhaseInitFailed is set when the init Job exits non-zero.
374		PhaseInitFailed = "InitFailed"
375		// PhaseBudgetExceeded is set when the project's absolute cost cap is exceeded.
376		PhaseBudgetExceeded = "BudgetExceeded"
377		// PhaseRunning is set when dispatch is actively proceeding.
378		PhaseRunning = "Running"
379		// PhasePushLeaseFailed is set when a push Job rejects due to --force-with-lease
380		// mismatch (Phase 3 D-B6). Recovery: kubectl annotate project
381		// tideproject.k8s/bypass-push-lease=true (mirrors D-D4 bypass-budget pattern).
382		PhasePushLeaseFailed = "PushLeaseFailed"
383		// PhasePushLeakBlocked is set when a push Job exits with code 10 (gitleaks
384		// finding — envelope.reason=leak-detected). Distinct from PhasePushLeaseFailed
385		// (exit-11) so the project_controller switch can fire
386		// tide_secret_leak_blocked_total only on the leak-class outcome (Phase 4 D-W1).
387		// Recovery: operator inspects the diff and either drops the leaked secret
388		// artifact or rotates the leaked credential, then clears the phase.
389		PhasePushLeakBlocked = "PushLeakBlocked"
390		// PhaseComplete is the terminal success phase — set when all Milestones
391		// reach Succeeded and the final push Job lands (Phase 3 D-B2 #4).
392		PhaseComplete = "Complete"
393	)
394	
395	// ProjectStatus defines the observed state of Project.
396	// PERSIST-02 / Pitfall 4: NO aggregate schedule fields here.
397	type ProjectStatus struct {

414		// lease failure counter (Phase 3 D-B6). PERSIST-02 / Pitfall 4: this is a

419		// BoundaryPush records the bounded auto-retry state of the project-level

421		// non-terminal BoundaryPushed condition for observability + bounded recovery.
422		// +optional

17	// Command claude-subagent — Phase 3 real-Claude swap of stub-subagent. Thin
18	// shim: load EnvelopeIn → anthropic.Run → write EnvelopeOut. Ignores the
19	// envelope's Dev struct (PATTERNS.md line 442 / D-F1).
20	package main
21	
22	import (
23		"context"
24		"encoding/json"
25		"flag"
26		"fmt"
27		"io"
28		"os"
29		"os/signal"
30		"path/filepath"
31		"syscall"
32		"time"
33	
34		"github.com/go-git/go-git/v5/plumbing"
35		"github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/internal/harness"
36		"github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/internal/subagent/anthropic"
37		pkgdispatch "github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/pkg/dispatch"
38	)
39	
40	// parsePricingOverridesFromEnv reads TIDE_PRICING_OVERRIDES_JSON and parses it.
41	// An empty or missing env var returns a nil map (no overrides). An invalid JSON
42	// value logs a loud stderr warning and returns nil (bad override must not fail
43	// the session — Plan 14-05 validates at manager startup; this is defense in depth).
44	func parsePricingOverridesFromEnv(stderr io.Writer) map[string]pkgdispatch.PriceOverride {
45		raw := os.Getenv("TIDE_PRICING_OVERRIDES_JSON")
46		if raw == "" {
47			return nil
48		}
49		overrides, err := pkgdispatch.ParsePricingOverrides(raw)
50		if err != nil {
51			fmt.Fprintf(stderr, "pricing: invalid TIDE_PRICING_OVERRIDES_JSON, ignoring overrides: %v\n", err)
52			return nil
53		}
54		return overrides
55	}
56	
57	type anthropicRunner interface {
58		Run(ctx context.Context, in pkgdispatch.EnvelopeIn) (pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut, error)
59	}
60	
61	var newSubagent = func(
62		claudeBinary, workspaceRoot string,
63		pricingOverrides map[string]pkgdispatch.PriceOverride,
64	) anthropicRunner {
65		return anthropic.New(anthropic.Options{
66			ClaudeBinary:     claudeBinary,
67			WorkspaceRoot:    workspaceRoot,
68			PricingOverrides: pricingOverrides,
69		})
70	}
71	var ensureWorktreeFunc = harness.EnsureWorktree
72	var commitWorktreeFunc = func(worktreeDir, taskUID string) (plumbing.Hash, bool, error) {
73		return harness.CommitWorktree(worktreeDir, taskUID)
74	}
75	
76	func main() {
77		fs := flag.NewFlagSet("claude-subagent", flag.ExitOnError)
78		envelopePath := fs.String("envelope-path", "",
79			"path to EnvelopeIn JSON (default: $TIDE_ENVELOPE_PATH or /workspace/envelopes/$TIDE_TASK_UID/in.json)")
80		workspaceRoot := fs.String("workspace-root", "/workspace", "PVC mount root")
81		if err := fs.Parse(os.Args[1:]); err != nil {
82			os.Exit(2)
83		}
84		// Resolve envelope path: flag → $TIDE_ENVELOPE_PATH → per-task-uid default.
85		// This MUST match stub-subagent (cmd/stub-subagent/main.go) and the
86		// controller's write location, which is /workspace/envelopes/$TIDE_TASK_UID/in.json
87		// (the controller passes no --envelope-path arg, so it relies on this default).
88		// The previous hardcoded /workspace/envelopes/in.json default did not include
89		// the per-task-uid subdirectory, so the real claude planner/executor could never
90		// find its input envelope. Only surfaced in live real-Claude runs — CI exercises
91		// the stub, whose default already matched.
92		if *envelopePath == "" {
93			if p := os.Getenv("TIDE_ENVELOPE_PATH"); p != "" {
94				*envelopePath = p
95			} else {
96				*envelopePath = fmt.Sprintf("/workspace/envelopes/%s/in.json", os.Getenv("TIDE_TASK_UID"))
97			}
98		}
99		ctx, cancel := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), syscall.SIGTERM, syscall.SIGINT)
100		defer cancel()
101		os.Exit(run(ctx, *envelopePath, *workspaceRoot, os.Stdout, os.Stderr))
102	}
103	
104	// run is the testable entry point. Does NOT branch on env.Dev.TestMode.
105	//
106	//nolint:unparam // stdout is part of the shared subagent-binary (stdout, stderr) entry-point contract
107	func run(ctx context.Context, envelopePath, workspaceRoot string, stdout, stderr io.Writer) int {
108		outPath := filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(envelopePath), "out.json")
109		env, err := harness.ReadEnvelopeIn(envelopePath)
110		if err != nil {
111			return failOut(stderr, outPath, "", err, 2, "invalid-envelope")
112		}
113		if err := ensureWorktreeFunc(env, workspaceRoot, env.Branch); err != nil {
114			return failOut(stderr, outPath, env.TaskUID, err, 1, "worktree-setup-failed")
115		}
116		// D-02: read pricing overrides from TIDE_PRICING_OVERRIDES_JSON env.
117		// Invalid JSON logs a loud warning and falls back to compiled table (defense in depth).
118		pricingOverrides := parsePricingOverridesFromEnv(stderr)
119		out, runErr := newSubagent("claude", workspaceRoot, pricingOverrides).Run(ctx, env)
120		if runErr != nil {
121			fmt.Fprintf(stderr, "claude-subagent: %v\n", runErr)
122			out = failEnvelope(env.TaskUID, runErr, 1, "subagent-error")
123		}
124		if runErr == nil && env.Role == "executor" {
125			worktreeDir := filepath.Join(workspaceRoot, "worktrees", env.TaskUID)
126			hash, isEmpty, commitErr := commitWorktreeFunc(worktreeDir, env.TaskUID)
127			if commitErr != nil {
128				out = failEnvelope(env.TaskUID, commitErr, 1, "commit-failed")
129			} else if isEmpty {
130				out.ExitCode = 1
131				out.Result = "empty-diff"
132				out.Reason = "executor produced no changes in worktree"
133			} else {
134				out.Git = &pkgdispatch.GitOutput{HeadSHA: hash.String()}
135			}
136		}
137		if err := writeEnvelope(outPath, out); err != nil {
138			fmt.Fprintf(stderr, "claude-subagent: write out.json: %v\n", err)
139			return 2
140		}
141		return out.ExitCode
142	}
143	
144	func failEnvelope(taskUID string, err error, exitCode int, result string) pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut {
145		return pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut{
146			APIVersion: pkgdispatch.APIVersionV1Alpha1, Kind: pkgdispatch.KindTaskEnvelopeOut,
147			TaskUID: taskUID, ExitCode: exitCode, Result: result, Reason: err.Error(),
148			CompletedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
149		}
150	}
151	
152	func failOut(stderr io.Writer, outPath, taskUID string, err error, exitCode int, result string) int {
153		fmt.Fprintf(stderr, "claude-subagent: %v\n", err)
154		_ = writeEnvelope(outPath, failEnvelope(taskUID, err, exitCode, result))
155		return exitCode
156	}
157	
158	func writeEnvelope(path string, out pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut) error {
159		// Write the full envelope to the PVC (audit artifact + reporter Job input).
160		// This is UNCHANGED — out.json on the PVC carries ChildCRDs + verbose Result.
161		if err := harness.WriteEnvelopeOut(path, out); err != nil {
162			return err
163		}
164		// Write only the tiny TerminationStub to the termination message path.
165		// The stub carries ExitCode/Reason/Usage/HeadSHA and stays <4KB regardless
166		// of ChildCRDs or Result size (#11 root cause fix; T-09-03 mitigation).
167		stub := pkgdispatch.NewTerminationStub(out)
168		data, _ := json.Marshal(stub)
169		tp := os.Getenv("TIDE_TERMINATION_MESSAGE_PATH")
170		if tp == "" {
171			tp = "/dev/termination-log"
172		}
173		_ = os.WriteFile(tp, data, 0o644)
174		return nil
175	}

# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1
# Build the claude-subagent runtime image.
# Invoked from REPO ROOT:
#   docker build -t ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent:test \
#                -f images/claude-subagent/Dockerfile .
#
# Stage 1: compile a static Linux Go binary.
FROM --platform=$BUILDPLATFORM golang:1.26-alpine@sha256:a6a091eac01ceac4b97496fe2957a49b6cdd83365337d5f46f6f73710424e805 AS builder
ARG TARGETOS
ARG TARGETARCH

WORKDIR /workspace

# Cache the dependency layer before copying source.
COPY go.mod go.sum ./
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/go/pkg/mod go mod download

# Copy only what the binary needs (see .dockerignore for exclusions).
COPY pkg/dispatch/ pkg/dispatch/
COPY internal/harness/ internal/harness/
COPY internal/subagent/ internal/subagent/
COPY pkg/git/ pkg/git/
COPY cmd/claude-subagent/ cmd/claude-subagent/

# Static binary: no CGO, stripped symbols for a smaller image.
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/go-build --mount=type=cache,target=/go/pkg/mod \
    CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=${TARGETOS:-linux} GOARCH=${TARGETARCH} go build \
      -ldflags="-s -w" \
      -o /out/claude-subagent \
      ./cmd/claude-subagent

# Stage 2: Claude Code CLI runtime image.
# node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732 is required because the claude CLI is a Node binary. The CLI
# version is pinned (Pitfall 3 — RESEARCH.md: never npm install without an
# explicit @<version>); pin tracks the planner's choice on the STACK floor of
# v2.1.139. The signed-token credential is injected at K8s Pod-spec time by
# the credproxy sidecar (Phase 2 D-C1..C4) — never baked into the image.
# Host configuration directories are not bind-mounted (CLAUDE.md anti-pattern).
#
# git IS required at the executor (task) level: EnsureWorktree shells out to the
# git CLI's `git worktree add` (pkg/git/worktree.go — go-git/v5 does not expose
# linked worktrees). Clone still uses go-git's pure-Go HTTP transport, but the
# per-Task worktree the executor authors into needs the real git binary, so
# node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732 (Debian) gets git installed. Prior to Phase 10 no executor ever
# ran end-to-end, so this gap stayed latent.
FROM node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732

RUN apt-get update \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends git \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
    # The per-Project repo (/workspace/repo.git) is cloned by the tide-push Job
    # (uid 65532) but the executor runs as uid 1000 — git 2.35+ refuses a repo
    # it does not own ("detected dubious ownership"). The pod is the trust
    # boundary (hermetic, single-purpose), so mark all repos safe at the system
    # level. This covers our `git worktree add` shell-out AND any git the claude
    # CLI itself runs inside the worktree (whose .git points back to repo.git).
    && git config --system --add safe.directory '*'

RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.npm npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code@2.1.142

COPY --from=builder /out/claude-subagent /usr/local/bin/claude-subagent

# Run as non-root UID 1000 — matches D-G3 subagent harness UID requirement.
USER 1000

ENTRYPOINT ["/usr/local/bin/claude-subagent"]
